claims.claim: The ABSE does not consider the influence of load current on equivalent circuit model parameters, making it unsuitable for SOAP estimation in theory.
claims.quote: The ABSE does not consider the influence of load current on the equivalent circuit model parameters, so it is not suitable for SOAP estimation in theory.

claims.claim: The ABSE algorithm error source is that its identified Rp is much higher than the actual value when the battery is charged or discharged at a high current.
claims.quote: the ABSE algorithm error source lies in its identified Rp being much higher than the actual value when the battery is charged/discharged at a high current.

claims.claim: With aging of the Li-ion battery, the SOC and SOAPSOAB estimation errors of the ABSE increase.
claims.quote: with the aging of the Li-battery, the SOC and SOAPSOAB estimation errors of the ABSE increase.

claims.claim: The battery SOAPSOAB decreases significantly after Uout reaches the border of the safe operating area (Uout,max) during the TSOAP-second measurement period.
claims.quote: the battery SOAPSOAB decreases significantly after Uout reaches the border of the safe operating area (Uout,max), which is the reason for the strong oscillation of the SOAPch profiles when the SOC is high.

claims.claim: The improved ABSE-based open-loop battery model achieves a voltage estimation error of less than 35 mV in the entire UDDS discharging process and less than 5 mV in the entire 0.5C CCCV charging process.
claims.quote: the absolute value of the voltage estimation error is less than 35 mV in the entire UDDS discharging process and less than 5 mV in the entire 0.5C CCCV charging process.

claims.claim: Overcharge fault causes the serial resistance to increase to four times larger than the initial value, which is the most severe influence on both serial and parallel resistors among the three fault modes tested.
claims.quote: Overcharge obviously has the most severe inﬂuence on both serial and parallel resistors. The serial resistance is four times larger than the initial value.

claims.claim: Over-discharge and low-temperature faults both result in serial resistance approximately 1.5 times larger than the initial value, compared to four times for overcharge.
claims.quote: Over-discharge and low-temperature cells had a smaller serial resistance, about 1.5 times larger than the initial one.

claims.claim: In over-discharged cells, the serial resistance is uniform throughout the entire depth of discharge range, whereas in low-temperature cells the serial resistance is dependent on depth of discharge.
claims.quote: The serial resistance of an over-discharged cell is uniform throughout the entire depth of discharge (DOD) range and that of the low-temperature one is dependent on DOD.

claims.claim: Normal overcharge process may induce multiple degradation mechanisms: part of the degradation is caused by high temperature (ICA pattern 1) and the other part results from pure overcharge (ICA pattern 4), with high temperature contributing the majority of degradation in the general case.
claims.quote: the normal overcharge process may induce multiple degradation mechanisms: part of the degradation is caused by high temperature (pattern1) and the other part results from pure overcharge (pattern 4). High temperature contributes the majority of degradation in general case.

claims.claim: When the overcharge current rate was reduced to 0.1 C to limit temperature rise, a new ICA pattern (pattern 4) was observed in which peak I increases first and peak II decreases, which was not observed at the standard 1 C rate where temperature rose up to 65 °C.
claims.quote: In small rate test, peak ① II shown in Fig. 10 increases at ﬁrst and peak ② II decreases. This induces a new curve pattern and it is deﬁned as pattern 4. Compared with the results from Fig. 8, we put forward our standpoint that the normal overcharge process may induce multiple degradation mechanisms

claims.claim: Mathematical battery models provide inaccurate results in the order of 5%-20% error and cannot represent dynamic information correctly.
claims.quote: these models cannot represent the dynamic information correctly and provide inaccurate results in the order of 5%-20% error.

claims.claim: Under the HPPC test, the Three RC Network Model achieved the lowest average voltage relative error (0.1068%) and the 3rd Order RC Model with One-State Hysteresis achieved the second lowest (0.2300%), while the Nonlinear RC Model had the highest average voltage relative error (1.2211%).
The PNGV model produces long-term accumulation of voltage on the capacitor, which increases the model voltage error over long simulation times.
claims.quote: the PNGV model produces long-term accumulation of voltage on the capacitor, which increases the model voltage error.

claims.claim: The Nonlinear RC model cannot reflect the polarization phases of the Li-ion battery, which limits its accuracy in representing dynamic characteristics.
claims.quote: The Nonlinear RC model does not accurately reflect the dynamic characteristics of the battery because this model cannot reflect the polarization phases of the Li-ion battery. Therefore, its accuracy is limited.

claims.claim: Under the ECE15 test, the Three RC Network Model achieved the lowest average voltage relative error (0.1831%) and maximum error (1.2663%), while the Shepherd Model had the highest maximum voltage relative error (2.8041%).
